title: tetraceratium stocksii
intro: Tetraceratium stocksii, also known as the "False Earthstar," is a type of fungus belonging to the order Pezizales. It is a rare, earthstar-like species found in Europe and North America, valued for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The medicinal properties of Tetraceratium stocksii include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Tetraceratium stocksii is often used in combination with other medicinal plants to create salves, tinctures, and infusions that promote wound healing and overall well-being.
